(姓)|直]]（おちのあたい）である。[[白村江の戦い]]で捕虜になったが、[[観音菩薩]]の霊験により無事帰還することができ、観音菩薩を奉じて寺を建てたという話が[[日本現報善悪霊異記|日本霊異記]]に載せられている。支配力が低下する中、[[仏教]]によって在地支配を再構築しようとした様子が伺われる（建立された古代寺院跡の場所は不明。）。 伊予史上では、[[神護景雲]]元年（[[767年]]）に献物により叙位された越智郡大領の越智直飛鳥麻呂と越智直国益が最初である。[[天暦]]2年（[[948年]]）には[[伊予国]]から海賊平定の功により[[越智用忠]]への叙位が認められており[[承平天慶の乱]]で政府軍側に立って戦ったことがわかる。[[永延]]元年（[[987年]]）[[東三条第]]で[[相撲]]があり、[[越智常世]]が伊予より助手として参加するなど、その後も史上には登場するものの、越智為保を伊予追捕使に任命[[長保]]4年（[[1002年]]）以降、越智氏が伊予の表舞台に登場することはない。 [[寛仁]]2年（[[1018年]]）に、[[伊予国|伊予守]][[源頼光]]が[[藤原道長]]に[[土御門殿]]の家具一切を献上したとあり、11.[[12世紀]]から国営支配の実権を握っていたのは[[在庁官人]]と呼ばれた地方豪族だったことからも、[[源氏]]は伊予守時代に越智氏と何らかの関係性が考えられ、源頼光が[[清和源氏]]三代目で、異母弟に[[源頼親]]が居ることや、越智氏のその後が表立って伊予で確認出来ない時期的なことからも、活躍の場を大和に移したとしても不思議ではない。 [[河野氏]]の記した『[[予章記]]』などで語られることの多い越智氏だが、[[孝霊天皇]]の第三[[皇子]][[彦狭島命|伊予皇子]]の第三[[王 (皇族)|王子]]を祖とするなどの粉飾が加えられていたりと信憑性に乏しく、越智氏自体の歴史は分からないことが多い。一般に越智氏系譜は河野氏系譜と混同視されており、疑問を投げかける声は少なくない。 [[今治市]][[大三島町]]の[[大山祇神社]]によれば河野氏を分家した後も代々ここの神職（大祝職）を担い、鎌倉時代には家名を大祝氏と名を変え現在これを担う[[三島家 (大山祇神社社家)|三島家]]へと姿を変えたとされている。また、越智の姓は現在でも今治市で最も多い姓として現代に残されている。なお、カバネの制度が残っていた明治初期の公文書では、[[伊藤博文]]が「越智宿禰博文」と姓（カバネ）と諱（いみな）を記載している。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|24em}} == 参考文献 == * 愛媛県の歴史―県史38（[[山川出版社]]、[[2003年]]） ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=姓氏 : 姓氏研究の決定版|publisher=[[秋田書店]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == {{div col||10em}} * [[物部氏]] * [[高市氏]] * [[新居氏]] * 別宮氏 * [[河野氏]] * [[橘氏 (伊予国)]] * [[予章記]] * [[越智氏 (大和国)]] {{div col end}} == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=越智氏考 |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html 武家家伝{{mdash}}越智氏] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おちうし}} revision.text:
[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|qq=|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|qq=|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|qq=|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|qq=|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|qq=|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|qq=|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|qq=|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|qq=|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}}

([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|qq=|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|qq=|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|qq=|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|qq=|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|qq=|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|qq=|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|qq=|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|qq=|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}}

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}}

According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}}

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}}

Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan]
