Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}

{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636555]] Wikidata name: Ochi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ([[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a [[:en:Clan|clan]] with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent [[:en:Gōzoku|local ruling clans]] in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]], originating in [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]] (present-day [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 [[:en:Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|World Encyclopedia]]|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the [[:en:Nankaidō|Nankaidō]] region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s [[:en:Kokufu|provincial government]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|provincial temple]]. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] but returned safely thanks to the blessings of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon]], to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ([[:en:Tenryaku|Tenryaku]] 2) for subduing pirates during the [[:en:Tengyō no Ran|Jōhei Tengyō War]]. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ([[:en:Chōhō (era)|Chōhō]] 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]] as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

([[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." Alternatively, Ochiō
([[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}

{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan}} with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local ruling clans}} in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, originating in {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}} (present-day {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 {{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=World Encyclopedia}}|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the {{ill|Nankaidō|en|Nankaidō|WD=Q1376747|lt=Nankaidō}} region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko}} system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s {{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=provincial government}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=provincial temple}}. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} but returned safely thanks to the blessings of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon}}, to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ({{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun}} 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku}} 2) for subduing pirates during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei Tengyō War}}. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ({{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō}} 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi}} as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

({{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ({{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})

{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)

{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)

The district became the site of Iyo’s {{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=provincial government}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=provincial temple}}. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} but returned safely thanks to the blessings of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon}}, to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ({{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun}} 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku}} 2) for subduing pirates during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei Tengyō War}}. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ({{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō}} 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi}} as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan}} with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local ruling clans}} in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, originating in {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}} (present-day {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 {{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=World Encyclopedia}}|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the {{ill|Nankaidō|en|Nankaidō|WD=Q1376747|lt=Nankaidō}} region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko}} system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s {{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=provincial government}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=provincial temple}}. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} but returned safely thanks to the blessings of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon}}, to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ({{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun}} 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku}} 2) for subduing pirates during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei Tengyō War}}. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ({{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō}} 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi}} as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

({{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." ({{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Prince Hikosashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}

{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Yano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan}} with the name "Ochi." ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Mishima family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan}} with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local ruling clans}} in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, originating in {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}} (present-day {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 {{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=World Encyclopedia}}|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the {{ill|Nankaidō|en|Nankaidō|WD=Q1376747|lt=Nankaidō}} region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko}} system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s {{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=provincial government}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=provincial temple}}. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} but returned safely thanks to the blessings of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon}}, to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ({{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun}} 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku}} 2) for subduing pirates during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei Tengyō War}}. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ({{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō}} 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Mishima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi}} as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Q122746984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)

The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku|1=Tenryaku}} 2) for subduing pirates during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei Tengyō War|1=Tengyō no Ran}}. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ({{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō|1=Chōhō (era)}} 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Q123407803|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Yano clan|ja|矢野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yano clan|1=Q27501003|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27501003}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Mishima family|ja|三島家 (薩摩藩士の家系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima family|1=Q122746984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122746984}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai)
{{ill|Imai clan|ja|今井氏#その他の今井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imai clan|1=Imai Clan (Suwa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24871105}} (Samurai) }} '''Ochi clan''' (越智氏) refers to a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=clan|1=Clan}} with the name "Ochi." The Ochi clan was one of the prominent {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local ruling clans|1=Gōzoku}} in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, originating in {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}} (present-day {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}).{{cite kotobank|word=越智氏|author=山内 譲|encyclopedia=改訂新版 {{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|WD=Q5698601|lt=World Encyclopedia|1=Heibonsha World Encyclopedia}}|accessdate=2024-03-10}} Some theories link the clan's origins to its association with the development of the {{ill|Nankaidō|en|Nankaidō|WD=Q1376747|lt=Nankaidō|1=Nankaidō}} region, but this connection remains unverified. == Overview == The Ochi clan is said to have originated in {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern {{ill|Kokubu, Imabari|ja|今治市国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the late 4th century, the establishment of the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} system by the Yamato court led to the creation of five Kuni no miyatsuko in western Ehime, including the Ochi clan. The clan’s founder is traditionally considered {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, grandson of Ōshinkawa no Mikoto of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage (『{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko no Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}』). According to the 『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』, Ochi no Atai (later Ochi Sukune) was a {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}} clan descending from {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.{{cite web|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/file/1256162.html|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku|website=National Archives of Japan|accessdate=2024-03-10}} During the early 8th century, the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} reorganized the administrative divisions into fourteen districts, designating the former Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko territory as "Ochi District." The district became the site of Iyo’s {{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=provincial government|1=Kokufu}} and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}}. Archaeological investigations near Kokubu have suggested a connection between the Ochi clan and the provincial government, but conclusive evidence is lacking. The earliest recorded figure of the Ochi clan is Ochi no Atai, a district governor of Ochi District. According to the 『{{ill|Nihon Ryōiki|ja|日本霊異記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Ryōiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国現報善悪霊異記}}』, he was captured during the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}} but returned safely thanks to the blessings of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon|1=Avalokiteśvara}}, to whom he later dedicated a temple. While the location of this ancient temple remains unknown, it reflects an effort to reassert local authority through Buddhism. In the history of Iyo, the first documented Ochi clan members were Ochi no Atai Asukamaro and Ochi no Atai Kunimaru, who were promoted in 767 ({{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 1) for their tribute offerings. Later, Ochi Mochitada received official recognition in 948 ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku|1=Tenryaku}} 2) for subduing pirates during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei Tengyō War|1=Tengyō no Ran}}. Despite occasional mentions of the Ochi clan in historical records, the clan disappeared from Iyo’s political scene after Ochi Tamemori’s appointment as a military commissioner in 1002 ({{ill|Chōhō (era)|en|Chōhō (era)|WD=Q1324844|lt=Chōhō|1=Chōhō (era)}} 4). == Legacy and Decline == The Ochi clan’s decline is marked by the increasing influence of the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} in Iyo Province. While sources like 『{{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}}』 provide genealogies linking the Ochi and Kōno clans, these accounts are often embellished with myths, such as tracing the Ochi lineage to Emperor Kōrei’s grandson. Such claims have been met with skepticism due to their lack of historical credibility. According to the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} in modern {{ill|Ōmishima, Imabari|ja|今治市大三島町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmishima, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Ochi clan became the shrine’s hereditary priests, later adopting the surname {{ill|Mishima|ja|三島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima|1=Q123407803|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407803}}. Today, "Ochi" remains a common surname in Imabari City. Notably, during the early Meiji era, documents recorded political leader {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi|1=Itō Hirobumi}} as "Ochi Sukune Hirobumi." == References == {{Reflist|24em}} == Bibliography == * ''History of Ehime Prefecture – Prefectural History 38'', {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}, 2003. ISBN 4-634-32380-X * {{Citation|和書|last=丹羽|first=基二|author-link=丹羽基二|others=[[樋口清之]]監修|date=1970-7|title=Surnames: The Definitive Study of Japanese Family Names|publisher=[[Akita Shoten]]|isbn=4253002099|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == {{div col||10em}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} * {{ill|Takaichi clan|ja|高市氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaichi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nii clan|ja|新居氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nii clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Bekku clan|ja|別宮氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekku clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|橘氏 (伊予国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544307}} * {{ill|Yoshōki|ja|予章記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshōki|1=Yoshōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371102}} * {{ill|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja|越智氏 (Yamato Province )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan (Yamato Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{div col end}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www17.ocn.ne.jp/~kanada/1234-7-15.html |title=Study on the Ochi Clan |date=20080105084406}} * [http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/oti_k.html Bushi Genealogies — Ochi Clan] {{translated page|ja|越智氏|version=103852764|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:越智氏]] [[ko:오치씨]] {{wikidata link|Q11636555}} [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Ochi Clan]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] [[ja:越智氏]][[ko:오치씨]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} {{otheruses|the Ochi clan of Iyo Province|a branch of the Yamato Minamoto clan|Ochi clan (Yamato Province)}} {{Japanese clans (ancient clans) | Name = Ochi clan | image = | Clan name = Ochi [[Sukune]] | Founder = {{ill|Ochinomikoto|ja|小致命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochinomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
({{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} lineage, {{ill|Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}The Ochi clan descends from Mononobe’s Ochinomikoto, who was appointed as the Ochi Kuni no Miyatsuko (Governor of Ochi County) and controlled Ochi District under the title "Atai." In November of 835 ({{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 2), his descendant Ochi Hiroshige was granted the surname Sukune from Atai (『{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}』). {{Sfn|丹羽|1970|p=355}})
Alternatively, Ochiō
({{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}’s third prince, {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487957}}’s third son?) | Origin = | Ancestor = | Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|lt=Shinbetsu|lt=Divine lineage|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine lineage|qq=}}
Alternatively {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | Hometown = {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}, {{ill|Ochi-go|ja|越智郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi-go|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Figures = | Descendants = {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}} (Samurai)
